DISCOVERY 301, JAN-23-2025 RE: MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS JIAMEI ZHOU, CHUXIN ZHOU, XIAOTING LIU, AND ZISHAO ZHOUS FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEFENDANTS SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NUMBERS 2, 5, 6, 7, 27, 31, 35, 39, 43, 47, 51, 55, 58, 62, 67, 78. THE MATTER IS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS LAWRENCE AND ANNIE YANG'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO CERTAIN SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST EMAIL IT TO CONTESTDEPT301TR@SFTC.ORG. calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-27-2025 Vacated

Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Jiamei Zhou, Chuxin Zhou, Xiaoting Liu, And Zishao Zhous Further Responses To Defendants Special Interrogatories Numbers 2, 5, 6, 7, 27, 31, 35, 39, 43, 47, 51, 55, 58, 62, 67, 78
Off Calendar Jan-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANTS LAWRENCE YANG , AN INDIVIDUAL, and ANNIE YANG'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Jiamei Zhou, Chuxin Zhou, Xiaoting Liu, And Zishao Zhous Further Responses To Defendants Special Interrogatories Numbers 2, 5, 6, 7, 27, 31, 35, 39, 43, 47, 51, 55, 58, 62, 67, 78.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Thomas W. J. Purtell, Esq., Law Office of, (415) 722-6291, for Plaintiffs.
Linda N. Hwee, Esq., of Bledsoe, Diestel, Treppa & Crane LLP, (415) 981-5411, for Defendants.

The matter is ARGUED. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants Lawrence and Annie Yang's motion to compel plaintiffs to provide further responses to certain special interrogatories and for monetary sanctions is denied in its entirety. The sole issue raised in the defendants' initial memorandum is the plaintiffs' use of the very common phrase "including, but not limited to." Defendants provide no authority or explanation why the use of that phrase is problematic in the context of plaintiffs' discovery responses or that they made anything me than a perfunctory effort to address their concerns with the use of that phrase. This is particularly troubling in light of the large amount of monetary sanctions sought by defendants. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HK)
